'''''UFC 278: Usman vs. Edwards 2''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on August 20, 2022, at [[Vivint Arena]] in [[Salt Lake City]], [[Utah]], United States.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2022/06/11/ufc-278-announced-salt-lake-city-kamaru-usman-vs-leon-edwards-headlines/|title=UFC 278 announced for Salt Lake City, Kamaru Usman vs. Leon Edwards headlines|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2022-06-11|access-date=2022-06-11}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's second visit to [[Salt Lake City]], following [[UFC Fight Night: Rodríguez vs. Caceres]] in August 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc4.com/sports/ufc-278-heads-to-salt-lake-city-with-usman-vs-edwards-2/|title=UFC 278 heads to Salt Lake City with Usman vs. Edwards 2 this summer|publisher=abc4.com|author=Vivian Chow|date=2022-06-13|access-date=2022-06-16}}</ref>

A [[UFC Welterweight Championship]] bout between current champion (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: American Top Team vs. Blackzilians]] welterweight winner) [[Kamaru Usman]] and [[Leon Edwards]] headlined the event.<ref name="UsmanEdwards">{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/6/11/23164282/ufc-278-set-for-salt-lake-city-with-kamaru-usman-vs-leon-edwards-2-headlining|title=UFC 278 set for Salt Lake City with Kamaru Usman vs. Leon Edwards 2 headlining|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Staff|date=2022-06-11|access-date=2022-06-11}}</ref> The pairing previously met at [[UFC on Fox: dos Anjos vs. Cowboy 2]] in December 2015 where Usman defeated Edwards by unanimous decision.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2015/12/ufc-on-fox-17-results-kamaru-usman-grinds-out-leon-edwards|title=UFC on FOX 17 results: Kamaru Usman grinds out Leon Edwards|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=John Morgan|date=2015-12-19|access-date=2022-06-12}}</ref> 

A middleweight bout between former [[List of Strikeforce champions#Middleweight Championship|Strikeforce]] and [[UFC Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] [[Luke Rockhold]] and former title challenger [[Paulo Costa]] was expected to take place at [[UFC 277]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2022/05/08/luke-rockhold-vs-paulo-costa-lined-ufc-277-july|title=Luke Rockhold vs. Paulo Costa lined up for UFC 277 in July|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2022-05-08|access-date=2022-05-26}}</ref> However, the bout was postponed to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/05/paulo-costa-vs-luke-rockhold-moves-from-ufc-277-to-august-20-fight-booking-announcement|title=Paulo Costa vs. Luke Rockhold shifts from UFC 277 to Aug. 20 event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2022-05-26|access-date=2022-05-26}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between Victor Altamirano and Jake Hadley was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mma.uno/victor-altamirano-vs-jake-hadley-agregado-a-ufc-278/|title=Victor Altamirano vs. Jake Hadley added to UFC 278|publisher=mma.uno|author=Steve Duncan|date=2022-05-26|access-date=2022-05-27}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> However, after Hadley pulled out of the bout due to injury he was replaced by Daniel Lacerda.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mymmanews.com/daniel-da-silva-and-victor-altamirano-look-to-secure-first-ufc-win-at-ufc-279/|title=Daniel da Silva and Victor Altamirano look to secure first UFC win at UFC 279|publisher=mymmanews.com|author=Bjorn Morfin|date=2022-06-16|access-date=2022-06-16}}</ref>

A women's flyweight bout between [[Miranda Maverick]] and [[Shanna Young]] was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{cite web|date=2022-06-30|title=Miranda Maverick vs. Shanna Young booked for UFC 278 in Salt Lake City|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/06/ufc-278-news-miranda-maverick-vs-shanna-young-salt-lake-city|access-date=2022-08-19|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref> However, the bout was cancelled on the day of the weigh-ins as Young was hospitalized due to weight cut issues.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/8/19/23312680/miranda-maverick-vs-shanna-young-scrapped-from-ufc-278|title=Miranda Maverick vs. Shanna Young scrapped from UFC 278|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2022-08-19|access-date=2022-08-19}}</ref> They were rescheduled for [[UFC Fight Night 214]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Gabriel Gonzalez|date=2022-08-24|title=UFC: Maverick vs Young rebooked for November 5, Miranda Maverick signs new contract|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2022/08/24/ufc-maverick-vs-young-rebooked-november-05-maverick-signs-new-contract/|access-date=2022-08-24|publisher=cagesidepress.com}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bloodyelbow.com/platform/amp/2022/8/21/23314824/ufc-278-post-fight-bonuses-leon-edwards-earns-50k-for-stunning-head-kick-ko-of-kamaru-usman-mma-news|title=UFC 278 post-fight bonuses: Leon Edwards earns $50k for stunning head kick KO of Kamaru Usman|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Kristen King|date=2022-08-21|access-date=2022-08-21|archive-date=2022-08-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220821054702/https://www.bloodyelbow.com/platform/amp/2022/8/21/23314824/ufc-278-post-fight-bonuses-leon-edwards-earns-50k-for-stunning-head-kick-ko-of-kamaru-usman-mma-news|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* '''Fight of the Night: Paulo Costa vs. Luke Rockhold'''
* '''Performance of the Night: Leon Edwards''' and '''Victor Altamirano'''

The following fighters received [[Crypto.com]] "Fan Bonus of the Night" awards paid in [[bitcoin]] of US$30,000 for first place, US$20,000 for second place, and US$10,000 for third place.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportskeeda.com/mma/news-fans-enraged-ufc-leon-edwards-misses-30k-crypto-com-bonus|title="Crypto fan voting stuff is nonsense" - Fans enraged with UFC after Leon Edwards misses out on $30K Crypto.com bonus|publisher=sportskeeda.com|author=Danish Ansari|date=2022-08-22|accessdate=2022-08-22}}</ref>
* '''First Place:''' Paulo Costa
* '''Second Place:''' Kamaru Usman
* '''Third Place:''' José Aldo

== Reported payout ==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[Utah Athletic Commission]]. The amounts do not include sponsor money, discretionary bonuses, viewership points or additional earnings. The total disclosed payout for the event was $2,542,000.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/9/7/23339924/ufc-278-salaries-kamaru-usman-500k-takes-home-top-disclosed-payday-in-knockout-loss|title=UFC 278 salaries: Kamaru Usman ($500k) takes home top disclosed payday in knockout loss|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2022-09-07|access-date=2022-09-07}}</ref>

* '''Leon Edwards''': $350,000 (no win bonus) def. Kamaru Usman: $500,000
* '''Paulo Costa''': $130,000 (includes $65,000 win bonus) def. Luke Rockhold: $200,000
* '''Merab Dvalishvili''': $198,000 (includes $99,000 win bonus) def. José Aldo: $400,000
* '''Lucie Pudilová''': $48,000 (includes $24,000 win bonus) def. Wu Yanan: $20,000
* '''Tyson Pedro''': $86,000 (includes $43,000 win bonus) def. Harry Hunsucker: $12,000
* '''Marcin Tybura''': $240,000 (includes $120,000 win bonus) def. Alexandr Romanov: $36,000
* '''Jared Gordon''': $94,000 (includes $47,000 win bonus) def. Leonardo Santos: $44,000
* Sean Woodson: $24,000 vs. Luis Saldaña: $14,000
* '''Ange Loosa''': $24,000 (includes $12,000 win bonus) def. A.J. Fletcher: $10,000
* '''Amir Albazi''': $32,000 (includes $16,000 win bonus) def. Francisco Figueiredo: $14,000
* '''Aori Qileng''': $24,000 (includes $12,000 win bonus) def. Jay Perrin: $12,000
* '''Victor Altamirano''': $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Daniel da Silva: $10,000
